The Genetic Royalty Revolution - VSL Script

HOOK

The $4 Trillion Medical Secret Hidden for 100 Years

"It's enough to turn every breakthrough cure into a personal payday." – Dr. Elias Vance, Former Big Pharma Insider and Molecular Biology Expert



Aaron Mitchell

For the first time in medical history, the word "cure" is becoming a real, investable business model.

We've been told, "There's no cure for cancer... no cure for Alzheimer's... no cure for diabetes."

But there's a side to this story you've never heard before.

A secret technology so powerful, it could eliminate disease forever... and create the biggest investment opportunity of our lifetime.

According to our guest today...

It all started decades ago...

When Big Pharma deliberately suppressed breakthrough gene therapy technologies to protect what he calls their "disease maintenance" business model.

Today, this technology has quietly advanced into something extraordinary.

Something that should have cured generations of suffering...

Instead...

For more than a century, it was locked away in research labs.

But now, thanks to a recent FDA policy shift...

It will soon be unleashed.

Our guest today says...

"Medicine is anything BUT limited to treatments."

And if you understand what's bound to happen next?

You could watch your own net worth soar as you collect "genetic royalties" on every cure created.



THE HIDDEN FORTUNE

According to Dr. Elias Vance, former senior researcher at one of the world's top pharmaceutical companies...

You and I are sitting on a "medical inheritance" worth trillions and counting.

Enough to cure every major disease known to mankind.

To put that in perspective, that's enough medical breakthrough power to make every American family healthier AND wealthier.

When it's unleashed, Dr. Vance predicts as early as this year...

It will silence Big Pharma's most powerful executives.

And hand a growing number of Americans a chance to profit from the end of disease.

So, where did this so-called "cure technology" come from?

Why was it kept secret, for more than a century?

And how can you claim your share of the genetic royalties?



Joining us now is our special guest, Dr. Elias Vance.

Dr. Vance has spent his fifteen-year career in the innermost circles of Big Pharma and cutting-edge medical research, making him one of the most medically connected men in America.

He holds a PhD in molecular biology from Johns Hopkins University.

He's published breakthrough research in peer-reviewed scientific journals.

He personally holds three biotech patents.

And for 15 years, he worked as a senior researcher inside one of the world's top 5 pharmaceutical giants...

Until he discovered something that made him walk away from a seven-figure salary.

Dr. Vance – it's an honor to have you with us.



Dr. Elias Vance

Glad to be with you, Aaron.

Aaron Mitchell

Now, moments before we went "on air," you told me we're about to see a profound transfer of wealth from Big Pharma to individual investors.

Yet very few people seem to be talking about this...

Dr. Elias Vance

Right. And there's good reason for that. This "genetic royalty" opportunity isn't about government handouts or get-rich-quick schemes. This is about positioning yourself to profit from the most important medical breakthrough since penicillin.

Aaron Mitchell

Interesting. So tell me...

If it's true that cures for major diseases have been suppressed for decades... how have they kept it quiet for so long?

Where does the technology come from?

And how can the average person profit?



THE $4 TRILLION "TRUST" NO ONE TALKS ABOUT

Dr. Elias Vance

Sure. I can answer those questions – starting with your first. Why all the secrecy?

Decades ago, pharmaceutical researchers discovered breakthrough gene therapy technologies that could actually cure diseases permanently.

You can go read the patents... the research papers... it's pretty technical stuff.

I got my PhD from Johns Hopkins. One of the top molecular biology programs in the nation. I promise you, we were taught that these technologies were "too experimental" or "too dangerous"... even though the science was sound.

It's been this big secret for years.

Yet over time, the potential of this technology has grown and grown... to the point where it could eliminate most major diseases and create trillions in new wealth.

Aaron Mitchell

Forgive me – but I'm hung up on this idea. Gene therapy that can actually cure diseases?

How?

Dr. Elias Vance

It didn't start out looking this powerful.

But the nature of gene editing technology is such that it gets exponentially more powerful as it advances...

Think of it like computer processing power... or internet speeds.

Each breakthrough builds on the last one, accelerating progress.

Aaron Mitchell

A technology this powerful. It's just... It's unfathomable that it's been hidden.

Dr. Elias Vance

I know... just to put it into perspective...

This technology could eliminate cancer, Alzheimer's, diabetes, heart disease, and dozens of other conditions that kill millions of people every year.

It could extend healthy human lifespan to 100+ years.

It could save the healthcare system trillions of dollars annually.

And it could create the biggest investment opportunity in modern history.

Now of course, I don't expect all of this to happen overnight. But the foundational technology is ready. It's just been suppressed.

This is not some kind of science fiction fantasy.

But it is a chance for the average American to become richer than they ever imagined. And a chance for a few little-known companies to become the new titans of medicine.

Aaron Mitchell

You know, for all the talk we've heard about "healthcare costs" and "incurable diseases"...

I'm surprised no one has mentioned this gene therapy revolution.

We hear about new cancer drugs, but they cost $100,000 and only extend life by a few months.

But this seems different...



WHY YOU HAVEN'T HEARD ABOUT THIS... UNTIL NOW

Dr. Elias Vance

No, no. This is different. Very different.

Those expensive cancer drugs you mentioned? They're designed to manage symptoms and extend life temporarily.

They're built on the old business model: keep patients alive and paying for as long as possible.

But gene therapy is different. It's designed to cure diseases permanently.

If Big Pharma released these cures, they would have destroyed their own business model.

As luck would have it, they failed for one specific reason, which I'll share in a moment.

The key thing to know is...

For the first time in our lives, the medical establishment is set to unlock this massive technology.

And it all ties to a pivotal FDA policy change that's flying under the radar.

Aaron Mitchell

I've got to say that sounds great, on paper. But I have so many questions.

Dr. Elias Vance

I figured you might. Take your time.

Aaron Mitchell

Well, assuming everything you've said is true... Why would Big Pharma allow something so disruptive to their business?

Dr. Elias Vance

Well as it turns out, they're not in control anymore.



A NEW MEDICAL PARADIGM FOR AMERICA?

Back in the 1920s, America was a young nation dealing with infectious diseases.

That's when the pharmaceutical industry established what I call the "Disease Maintenance Model."

[Image of early pharmaceutical companies]

Instead of curing diseases, they focused on managing symptoms with daily pills.

Aaron Mitchell

Daily pills instead of one-time cures? That's quite a business model.

Dr. Elias Vance

For them it was.

Their investment equated to billions in ongoing revenue.

While a one-time cure might be worth millions.

Aaron Mitchell

Wait. So, these companies had a financial incentive to avoid cures?

Dr. Elias Vance

Indeed. But there was more to it.

To maintain this system, they had to suppress competing technologies.

Aaron Mitchell

Ah – well. That explains a lot.

Dr. Elias Vance

No... And to be clear, this story is not about conspiracy theories.

You see...

Despite spending trillions on "research," Big Pharma retained control over the most valuable part.

For 100 years, they've held back the cure technologies.

But thanks to recent FDA policy changes, they will soon lose this monopoly.

Dr. Elias Vance

And just like we saw with those early pharmaceutical breakthroughs...

It's not Big Pharma that will profit most from this transition.

It's the investors that act on this opportunity.

Aaron Mitchell

And who might that be?

Dr. Elias Vance

I'm talking about the small biotech companies who developed the core gene editing platforms, and the regular folks who know how to invest in them before Wall Street catches on.

Only in this case, you don't have to become a scientist or understand complex biology.

You don't even have to risk your life savings.

With a small investment in the right companies, you could collect ongoing "genetic royalties" as this technology transforms medicine.

Aaron Mitchell

All that sounds incredible.

But there's a hole in this story...

What is this gene therapy technology exactly? And how do these "genetic royalties" work?

Far as I know, pharmaceutical companies aren't paying individual investors royalties.

Dr. Elias Vance

You're right...

But I'm talking about something far more valuable than traditional pharmaceutical investing.

Aaron Mitchell

Before we get to that... I'm wondering how exactly gene therapy creates "royalties."

At a biotech company?



THE MEDICAL INDUSTRY'S BEST-KEPT SECRET, HIDING IN PLAIN SIGHT

Dr. Elias Vance

No. Individual biotech companies only control pieces of the puzzle.

Take a look at what I discovered...

The real value is held by platform technology companies.

[Display showing gene editing platform companies]

These companies don't develop individual drugs...

They own the foundational patents that ALL gene therapy developers must license.

Aaron Mitchell

Woah — like a technology platform that everyone has to use?

Dr. Elias Vance

Exactly. Think of it like Qualcomm and smartphone technology.

Qualcomm doesn't make phones. But every smartphone manufacturer pays them royalties to use their core patents.

Aaron Mitchell

But how do we know these gene therapy platforms haven't just sold their technology already? You know...

I was just thinking about early internet companies.

Many of them sold out too early and missed the real wealth creation.

Dr. Elias Vance

Aaron, I can't vouch for every biotech company out there.

But these platform companies are different.

Unlike single-drug developers, they can't just sell their technology once. The patents are structured to generate ongoing royalties from every application.

That's a key reason why their value will compound over decades. But I believe all that's about to accelerate dramatically, starting as early as this year.

Aaron Mitchell

Now we're getting somewhere.

So we're NOT talking about buying individual drug companies... or hoping a specific clinical trial succeeds.

Dr. Elias Vance

That's right...

And it's not a traditional biotech investment where you're gambling on FDA approvals.

It's much more predictable than that.

That's why very few people know about this opportunity.

And here's the thing...

If you're over age 50, what happens next could be your final chance to build "generational wealth" while potentially benefiting from medical breakthroughs that could extend your own healthy lifespan.

That's why it's critical you know what's happening and how you can profit.

Aaron Mitchell

I'm beginning to see the big picture. But if this technology is as POWERFUL as you say... why have so few people heard about it?

Dr. Elias Vance

It's beyond the imagination of most people – including most doctors and investment professionals.

But Americans who stake even a modest claim – with a small investment – could see an explosion of wealth in the years ahead.

There will be surprising medical effects as well.

Aaron Mitchell

Medical effects. What do you mean by that?



BIG PHARMA'S HARSHEST CRITICS WILL SOON FALL SILENT

Dr. Elias Vance

The pharmaceutical industry could be completely transformed... becoming focused on actual cures instead of symptom management.

Companies that adapt could become heroes... saving millions of lives while creating tremendous wealth.

[Image showing medical transformation]

Or they could become obsolete overnight.

Aaron Mitchell

Big Pharma?? Completely transformed? That seems impossible.

Dr. Elias Vance

Look, Aaron, I can tell from the look on your face, what you're thinking...

Aaron Mitchell

It seems almost too good to be true.

But that's why I invited you to join us.

Over the past decade, we've seen dozens of "revolutionary medical breakthrough" predictions. And we've done our best to investigate them thoroughly.

But when we looked into your background, Dr. Vance...

An astounding number of your predictions have proven RIGHT.

In 2018, you identified a small gene therapy company called GeneSys as a potential acquisition target. 18 months later, it was bought by Merck for a 1,200% premium.

Then in 2020, you predicted that mRNA technology would become mainstream. At the time, most doctors had never heard of it. But then came the COVID vaccines.

Then in 2022, you called the FDA's policy shift toward gene therapy approvals. And that's exactly what happened in 2023.

When it comes to biotech breakthroughs—you've been remarkably prescient.

But your latest prediction? It's something else.

Dr. Elias Vance

I don't blame you if you think my new prediction sounds a bit ambitious.

Like I said.

No one has a reference point for this.

Because nothing like it has happened since the discovery of antibiotics.

Now this might sound like it's coming out of left field...

But I promise it will all make sense in a minute.

Do you remember when gene therapy was considered "experimental"?

Aaron Mitchell

I do recall that being the standard medical position for decades.

Dr. Elias Vance

Right. But in 2023, the FDA quietly changed their guidance. They said:

[Quote on screen]: "Gene and cell therapies hold significant promise to treat, or even cure, many intractable diseases..."

And this all ties back to our genetic royalty opportunity.

Of course, the mainstream media mostly ignored this policy shift.

And traditional investment advisors piled on too, saying, "Gene therapy is still too risky."

Not surprising...

Because these people rarely do deep research. All they do is repeat outdated conventional wisdom.

But have you ever wondered...

Where did our biggest medical breakthroughs come from?

Aaron Mitchell

I guess I never really thought about it systematically.

Dr. Elias Vance

Right. Let me give you some perspective.

Penicillin was discovered almost by accident in 1928...

[Historical image of Fleming's lab]

But it didn't become widely available until the 1940s. And when it did, it created enormous wealth for early pharmaceutical investors.

Insulin was discovered in 1922...

[Historical image of insulin discovery]

But the companies that commercialized it became some of the biggest pharmaceutical giants in history.

The polio vaccine was developed in the 1950s...

[Historical image of Salk vaccine]

And it made Jonas Salk a household name while creating tremendous value for pharmaceutical companies.

Aaron Mitchell

I see the pattern. Medical breakthroughs that seem impossible... eventually become standard treatment.

Dr. Elias Vance

Right, and to be clear, gene therapy advances are following the exact same pattern.



THE GOLD RUSH OF THE 21ST CENTURY?

Aaron Mitchell

Dr. Vance, I get it. This idea of a "medical revolution" makes sense historically. But I've got to be honest.

Everyone I know who's making real money in biotech? They've either gotten lucky with a single stock... or they've bought diversified biotech ETFs and held for decades.

Dr. Elias Vance

You're right, we haven't seen a REAL platform technology opportunity in our lifetime.

But the last time this happened in medicine?

So many people cashed in... it created entirely new industries.

[Historical wealth creation chart]

"Rags to riches" stories were common.

And now, it's about to happen, once again.

These gene editing platforms... they're the building blocks of every future cure for cancer, Alzheimer's, diabetes, heart disease, and dozens of other conditions.

They're the foundation that every biotech company will need to license.

Aaron Mitchell

Well, you put it that way, I guess "genetic royalties" don't seem so far-fetched.

Dr. Elias Vance

Right, and to be clear, you don't have to move to Silicon Valley or become a scientist to take advantage of this opportunity.

Aaron Mitchell

That's a relief.

But there's one thing I can't wrap my head around...

Why haven't we seen these genetic royalty companies take off already?

Dr. Elias Vance

That's a great question...

With a simple answer.

It takes massive scientific validation to prove a platform technology works.

Not just one successful treatment. Dozens of them.

And unfortunately, the FDA got in the way.

For the past 30 years, or so...

Washington backed traditional pharmaceutical approaches, focusing on symptom management rather than cures.

They were terrified of "experimental" gene therapy.

But the science has become undeniable.

Gene therapy works. It can cure diseases permanently. And the FDA can no longer justify blocking these breakthrough treatments.

Reuters: FDA Approves First Gene Therapy for Inherited Blindness New York Times: Gene Therapy Cures Sickle Cell Disease in Clinical Trial Science Magazine: CRISPR Gene Editing Shows Promise for Alzheimer's Treatment

And they made it next to impossible for small biotech companies to get their breakthrough treatments approved.

One former FDA insider says...

[Quote on screen]: "We have all these life-saving technologies sitting in labs. Incredibly, insanely, however the United States medical establishment has been locking them up."

Aaron Mitchell

So, all these breakthrough treatments were "locked up" by overly cautious regulators – for decades?

Dr. Elias Vance

You probably noticed...

The FDA chose to protect pharmaceutical industry profits... instead of protecting and curing the American public.

[News headlines]: Washington Post: FDA Slow to Approve Gene Therapies Despite Promising Results Wall Street Journal: Biotech Companies Struggle with Regulatory Hurdles STAT News: Gene Therapy Approvals Lag Behind Scientific Progress

Aaron Mitchell

Alright. I mean, I'm all for medical safety. But what kind of agency would delay life-saving treatments to protect industry profits?

Dr. Elias Vance

Look, I appreciate careful scientific review too. But these people were captured by Big Pharma interests.

To protect their "disease maintenance" business model, they strangled innovation. They delayed breakthrough treatments for decades.

Then they forced Americans to rely on expensive, ineffective symptom management drugs.

Capital Research reports:

[Quote on screen]: "Unbelievable as it may seem, breakthrough gene therapies that could cure major diseases have been delayed by regulatory capture for over two decades."

So, all this time – we've had these cure technologies right in our research labs.

Yet for years, we refused to approve them for patients who were dying.

Aaron Mitchell

You know, when you put it that way... I can't help but think of other countries that have been more aggressive with gene therapy approvals.

Dr. Elias Vance

That's right. Countries like the UK and Germany have been approving gene therapies that we've been blocking.

While we've been protecting Big Pharma profits, other nations have been saving lives with the same technologies.

Aaron Mitchell

I wish you hadn't mentioned that. It seems tragic.

Dr. Elias Vance

Well, don't lose hope just yet.



AMERICA'S FIRST-EVER GENETIC ROYALTY BOOM

Recently, the FDA established new "Fast Track" pathways for gene therapy approvals. Something that has NEVER happened before.

[News headline]: FDA Announces Streamlined Gene Therapy Approval Process

Now, I'm not saying these treatments will be available to everyone immediately.

But the regulatory barriers have been removed. And there are clear signs of what's coming.

Recently, FDA Commissioner Dr. Robert Califf said, "We're entering a new era where genetic medicines will transform how we treat disease."

Contrast that with traditional pharmaceuticals, which are designed to manage symptoms indefinitely.

Aaron Mitchell

It makes me think – how this could completely disrupt the pharmaceutical industry. Will this new approach help fix our broken healthcare system?

Dr. Elias Vance

There's no telling how transformative this will be. Anything is possible. We'll just have to wait and see.

But Aaron, if the current healthcare system's failures make you concerned? Brace yourself...

Because guess who's been profiting from keeping us sick?

BIG PHARMA!

In addition to charging massive prices for symptom management drugs...

They've systematically suppressed cure technologies that could eliminate their most profitable diseases.

Aaron Mitchell

Suppressed them in what way?

Dr. Elias Vance

They've bought up promising gene therapy companies and shut down their research programs.

They've lobbied the FDA to maintain restrictive approval processes.

They've spread fear about "experimental" treatments to keep patients dependent on their drugs.

[News headline]: Pharmaceutical Giants Acquire Gene Therapy Startups Only to Shelve Technology

Aaron Mitchell

That's... that's actually evil.

Dr. Elias Vance

It gets worse.

This reminds me of the early days of the internet, when established companies tried to suppress new technologies that threatened their business models.

But just like the internet revolution couldn't be stopped...

Neither can the gene therapy revolution.

Which is why smart investors are moving fast to position themselves in the platform companies that will power this transformation.

Aaron Mitchell

Wow – I mean – you put it that way, it's no wonder these genetic royalty opportunities exist.

So to recap: We've been sitting on cure technologies for decades. All the treatments we could possibly need to eliminate major diseases.

And meanwhile...

Our medical system has acted like these diseases are incurable!

Charging us enormous sums for symptom management while suppressing actual cures.

Dr. Elias Vance

Yes – but all that stops now.



BREAKING BIG PHARMA'S DECADES-OLD MONOPOLY

Recently, the FDA announced:

[Quote on screen]: "There are certain gene therapies where we have great, proven science. And we're no longer going to delay them because of industry pressure. We're going to let patients access these treatments."

So today, for the first time in decades, they're fast-tracking gene therapy approvals.

[Map of US showing gene therapy trials]

And green-lighting companies that will develop trillions of dollars' worth of cures, right here in America.

You can see on this map – the highlighted areas – where breakthrough gene therapy trials are happening. It adds up to roughly 400+ active trials across the U.S. And many of these are for diseases that have never had effective treatments before.

Aaron Mitchell

Well judging by what we've learned so far... It's about time we allowed these life-saving treatments!

There's just one problem.

Dr. Elias Vance

And what's that?

Aaron Mitchell

The FDA has been blocking gene therapy for decades.

They couldn't get breakthrough treatments approved under previous administrations.

They didn't revolutionize medicine in the 2010s... or make major diseases curable.

They couldn't even get simple gene therapies through clinical trials quickly.

So what's changed?

Dr. Elias Vance

You're right...

Most people don't realize...

Gene therapy researchers were sabotaged from day one.

Their first decade was wasted fighting regulatory red tape designed to protect Big Pharma.

Their second decade was destroyed by safety scares manufactured by pharmaceutical lobbying groups.

Their third decade was consumed by funding cuts and research restrictions.

Aaron Mitchell

So they never had a chance to prove what gene therapy could really do.

Dr. Elias Vance

Right. But this time is different.

Recently, breakthrough clinical trial results made a pivotal impact.



THIS SINGLE POLICY STRANGLED MEDICAL INNOVATION... BUT NOW IT'S HISTORY!

I don't want to get into the regulatory weeds.

But for decades, there's been an unwritten rule at the FDA: "First, do no harm to pharmaceutical profits."

But it's clear to me, its purpose was to prevent breakthrough treatments from reaching patients.

Have you ever wondered what happened to all the promising gene therapy research from the 1990s? The revolutionary treatments that could cure genetic diseases? The breakthrough approaches that could eliminate cancer?

Most of them disappeared...

Thanks to a web of regulatory barriers designed to protect Big Pharma's subscription business model.

Aaron Mitchell

Right. Most research funding went to symptom management drugs instead.

Dr. Elias Vance

And why does everything seem to be "managed" instead of "cured?" If you ever wondered why it's so expensive to treat cancer over here... while spending decades on symptom management. This is it. They don't want one-time cures.

For the past 50 years, regulatory capture has strangled us from within the medical establishment. They tied researchers down with endless bureaucracy. And they ushered in one expensive treatment failure after another.

Like cancer drugs that cost $100,000 and extend life by three months.

So they hollowed out medical innovation... And we wound up with treatments that barely work... diagnostic tests that take forever... and hospital systems that bankrupt families.

And these same captured regulators? They gave Big Pharma "veto power" to stop any breakthrough cure they wanted, at any time, for any reason.

Look...

Recently, a consulting firm put together a list of the top countries for medical innovation... based on how long it takes to bring breakthrough treatments to patients.

And guess where the United States ranks?

Aaron Mitchell

We spend more on healthcare than any other nation.

Ah, I'm guessing somewhere near the top.

Dr. Elias Vance

No!

[Chart]: United States Ranks Dead Last in Time to Approve Breakthrough Treatments

We came in dead last.

[World map highlighting various countries]

Aaron Mitchell

You've got to be kidding!

Dr. Elias Vance

Think about this...

When it comes to bringing life-saving gene therapies to patients...

Our country performs worse than countries with a fraction of our resources!

We have truly massive medical expertise here. We know how to develop cures. We have the best research institutions in the world.

But the regulatory system got corrupted by pharmaceutical industry influence.

Aaron Mitchell

That's just... brutal.

Dr. Elias Vance

You have no idea...

There's a gene therapy for sickle cell disease that's been ready for approval for 8 years. It could cure a disease that affects 100,000 Americans.

[US Map highlighting sickle cell treatment delay]

Then there's a breakthrough Alzheimer's treatment using gene editing. It's been stuck in regulatory review since 2019!

[US Map highlighting Alzheimer's treatment delay]

A gene therapy that could cure Type 1 diabetes has been waiting for FDA approval since 2020.

[US Map highlighting diabetes treatment delay]

Guess how long patients have been waiting for these life-saving treatments?

I don't know. 5 years... 10?

Dr. Elias Vance

Some have been waiting over a decade!

Aaron Mitchell

That's criminal.

Dr. Elias Vance

It's beyond criminal. It's a systematic suppression of medical progress to protect pharmaceutical profits.

But now...

That's all about to change.



AN HISTORIC TURNING POINT FOR MEDICINE

Aaron Mitchell

Of course not.

Dr. Elias Vance

You don't have to be a scientist or a biotech expert to profit from this opportunity.

That's right.

100 YEARS OF MEDICAL PROGRESS... IN THE NEXT DECADE

Why is that? Because for the first time in our lifetime, medicine is going to be based on actual cures instead of symptom management.

The whole idea of owning gene therapy platforms... it sounds complicated.

Right. Well, not to worry.

I'm not talking about doing anything complicated like directly licensing patents. You don't have to be rich or an accredited investor.

It's as simple as owning shares of a few specific stocks.

In fact, there are three ticker symbols you can type into any brokerage account that will give you a stake in three companies I believe will skyrocket in the years ahead.

Each with the potential to deliver 10x... 20x... or more as gene therapy goes mainstream. Conservatively speaking...

Sure...

Alright. So this company's platform is essential for developing gene therapies worth potentially $1 trillion.

How much are shares in this platform company worth?

Well, it depends.

During the regulatory suppression era... the platform had limited commercial value.

[News headlines about regulatory barriers]

Aaron Mitchell

In other words, the gene therapy revolution was blocked.

Dr. Elias Vance

That's right.

Aaron Mitchell

OK. In that case, platform company shares could be worth much less.

Dr. Elias Vance

Much less. Pennies on the dollar.

But now, the same platform technology could be worth a fortune!

And this is not hypothetical.



HISTORICAL WINDFALLS - COULD IT HAPPEN AGAIN?

This is precisely what happened with a company called Royalty Pharma.

Back in the early 2000s, when drug patents became more valuable, its royalty-based business model exploded. Its shares went from $10 to over $40. That's a gain of 300%... and it pays ongoing dividends.

That's a solid return, especially with dividend income.

Keep in mind, this was a mature company with established royalty streams. But this shows what's possible when you own royalty rights to breakthrough medical technologies.

I could show you more dramatic examples.

Take Gilead Sciences. Back in the 1990s, it was a small biotech company focused on antiviral drugs. When HIV treatments became essential, its shares leapt from $2 to over $100.

[Chart: Gilead Sciences (GILD), 5,000%+ Peak Gain]

What's that... 5,000% or more? That's life-changing wealth creation.

Dr. Elias Vance

Well, in rare moments like this, anything is possible.

There's a company called Vertex Pharmaceuticals.

It developed platform technology for treating genetic diseases.

Same story.

At one point, you could have bought shares for less than $15.

But when their gene therapy platform proved successful – it raced to over $300. Roughly 2,000% higher, over two decades.
